etymology.description: Original 𧵽: 网(網) (wǎng, 'net') + 貝(貝) (bèi, 'money cowrie') -> codified as buy -> 買 (simplified to phono: 羅(羅) (luó) + semantic: 貝)
etymology.interpretation: Although 買(mǎi, 'buy') originally used a net and cowrie to symbolize a transaction, it later evolved into a phono-semantic compound, using 羅(羅) (luó) as a phonetic component along with 貝(貝) (bèi, 'money cowrie') as the semantic element to maintain its association with purchasing goods.
etymology.simplification: 买 is a simplified form where 网(網) (wǎng, 'net') and 貝(貝) (bèi, 'money cowrie') were omitted, leaving the essential components of transaction indicated through simpler glyphs.

examples.chinese: 我要去商店買新衣服(我要去商店買新衣服)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ yào qù shāngdiàn mǎi xīn yīfú.
examples.english: I am going to the store to buy new clothes.

examples.chinese: 她喜歡買書(她喜歡買書)，因為她熱愛閱讀(因為她熱愛閱讀)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān mǎi shū, yīnwèi tā rè'ài yuèdú.
examples.english: She likes to buy books because she loves reading.

examples.chinese: 我們今天就可以買票(我們今天就可以買票)，以確保有位置(以確保有位置)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen jīntiān jiù kěyǐ mǎi piào, yǐ quèbǎo yǒu wèizhì.
examples.english: We can buy tickets today to ensure we have seats.
